name: Debug-Job

options.name: -BreakAll
options.description: Allows you to break immediately in the current location when the debugger attaches.

options.name: -Id
options.description: Specifies the ID number of a running job. To get the ID number of a job, run the `Get-Job` cmdlet.

options.name: -InstanceId
options.description: Specifies the instance ID GUID of a running job.

options.name: -Job
options.description: Specifies a running job object. The simplest way to use this parameter is to save the results of a `Get-Job` command that returns the running job that you want to debug in a variable, and then specify the variable as the value of this parameter.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies a job by the friendly name of the job. When you start a job, you can specify a job name by adding the JobName parameter, in cmdlets such as `Invoke-Command` and `Start-Job`.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Debugs a running background or remote job.
